meta.title: Mercedes-Benz OM611/OM613 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM611/OM613 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM611 and OM613 are the original CDI-era diesels — tough and high-mileage capable, but injector and fuel pump issues are well documented. Backed by 145+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on 145+ UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports.

rows.score: 16 (Most failures are well-understood repairs (injectors, fuel pumps, CPS))
20 (Parts widely available; common across many Mercedes models)
16 (Recon cost ~£2,200 against lowest host value ~£4,500 = 49% (45–59% band → 16))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The OM611/613 are robust, high-mileage CDI diesels — injector and fuel pump wear are the main weak points — buy with full service history and injector service records."
verdictRating.bestFor: High-mileage drivers seeking bulletproof Mercedes diesel longevity with simple mechanicals.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented injector or fuel pump service history on higher-mileage examples.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM611/OM613 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [MB-QUOTE].

cta.label: → Compare OM611/OM613 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does an OM611/OM613 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned OM611/OM613 typically costs £1,800–£2,800 supply only [MB-QUOTE], varying by displacement (2.2L vs 3.2L) and specific variant. Fitting adds £700–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the OM611/OM613 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: The OM611 and OM613 are considered among Mercedes' most durable diesel engines — many examples exceed 250,000 miles with proper maintenance. Injector and high-pressure fuel pump wear are the main concerns, but both are well-understood and repairable.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common OM611/OM613 failure?"
items.answer: Injector leak-off and high-pressure fuel pump failure are the most common issues — typically presenting as poor starting, rough idle, or loss of power. Crankshaft position sensor failure is also a known weak point, often causing sudden cut-out.

items.question: Fitment — "Which Mercedes-Benz models use the OM611/OM613?"
items.answer: The OM611 powers C-Class (W202/W203), E-Class (W210/W211), and the Sprinter van range. The OM612 2.7L appears in the ML270 CDI, while the OM613 3.2L is found in flagship S-Class and CL-Class models.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the OM611/OM613 last?"
items.answer: With regular servicing and timely injector maintenance, OM611/613 engines regularly exceed 250,000 miles — many examples from the late 1990s still run strongly today. Fuel pump wear is the main factor limiting ultimate lifespan.
